warnings: 1822 points (40.8%) had numerical failures, mostly at high g_D (>1.5) where the backend bounce action computation fails
395 points (8.8%) failed physical guards (CW condition or supercooling condition)
The backend produces RuntimeWarning for divide-by-zero in log at extreme parameter values; these are caught and recorded as numerical failures
Extremely large alpha values (up to 10^24) at very low vev (0.1 MeV) indicate strong supercooling; these points have f_peak_est below the PTA band
approximations: High-temperature expansion of finite-T effective potential valid in supercooling regime
Gaussian approximation for false vacuum fraction
Scalar loop contributions to CW potential neglected (gb_scalar = 0 in backend; subleading per pipeline documentation)
Renormalisation scale set to vev; no RG running included
f_peak_est is a template-independent order-of-magnitude estimate; the true spectral peak depends on the GW template used by the pta-agent

backend_choice.reason: Model is classically scale-invariant with single scalar field, masses proportional to vev, no tree-level mass term. Compatible per BACKEND_COMPATIBILITY.md.

g_D.notes: Dark gauge coupling; scanned in FOPT scan

y_psi.notes: Yukawa coupling; scanned in FOPT scan; constrained by CW condition y_psi < (3/2)^(1/4)*g_D and supercooling condition g_D > y_psi/sqrt(2)

v.notes: Dark Higgs vev; scanned in FOPT scan

beta_lambda.notes: CW beta function parameter; beta_lambda > 0 required for radiative symmetry breaking

m_chi.notes: Physical mass of the CW pseudo-dilaton (radial scalar mode)

m_psi.notes: Mass of each Dirac fermion mass eigenstate

gb_scalar.notes: Scalar self-coupling gb for backend; set to 0 in backend by default

xi.notes: T_dark / T_SM temperature ratio; assumes dark sector thermalised with SM

epsilon_kinetic_mixing.notes: Gauge kinetic mixing between U(1)_D and U(1)_Y; set to zero at tree level

lambda_HPhi.notes: Higgs portal coupling |H|^2|Phi|^2; set to zero
